evidence: src/lib/ root has 14 flat files (avg fan-in 1.1) — tips.ts (orphaned, 442 LOC), predictions.ts (orphaned, 391 LOC), mentions.ts (orphaned, 148 LOC), transcript.ts (orphaned, 175 LOC), review-findings.ts (orphaned, 112 LOC) are all dead code inflating the directory

src/reactive/ directory tree (20 files, 3 subdirs, 1654 LOC) is almost entirely orphaned — event-loop.ts (orphaned, 257 LOC), watermark-store.ts (orphaned, 90 LOC), 6 of 7 event-handlers are orphaned, multiple event-sources are orphaned. Only 3-4 files have any importers
src/lib/sources/providers/hooks/ has 2 files (arxiv.ts 24 LOC, kraken.ts 140 LOC) with zero importers — completely orphaned subdirectory

src/lib/ remaining 9 non-orphaned files have mixed concerns: agent-config.ts (configuration), spending-policy.ts (financial), budget-tracker.ts (financial), state.ts (session), test-quality-validator.ts (testing), improvement-utils.ts (self-improvement) — low cohesion

src/plugins/ has 22 files which is approaching flat-directory-overload territory, though all files follow consistent naming ({concern}-plugin.ts) and the concern is homogeneous (FrameworkPlugin implementations)
src/toolkit/ is well-organized: clear subdirs for tools/ and guards/, coherent types.ts + session.ts + sdk-bridge.ts at root — high fan-in (7.4) confirms these are genuinely core files
coupling_matrix shows clean dependency directions: toolkit/tools/ → toolkit/ (36 edges), plugins/ → src/ (21 edges) — no bidirectional coupling at directory level
src/adapters/ is well-structured with eliza/ and skill-dojo/ as clear adapter boundaries

package_organization.dimension_character: The codebase has a well-structured toolkit/ and adapters/ layer, but src/lib/ and src/reactive/ suffer from accumulated orphaned files and low cohesion. The lib/ root is a flat catch-all with 5+ orphaned files (1268 LOC of dead weight) and the remaining files span unrelated concerns. The reactive/ subsystem is ~85% orphaned, suggesting it was built speculatively or its integration was never completed. The hooks/ subdirectory is entirely dead code.
package_organization.score_rationale: Score of 68.5 reflects significant organizational debt concentrated in two areas: src/lib/ flat root with mixed concerns and orphaned files, and src/reactive/ as a largely dead subsystem. These issues inflate the codebase by ~2500 LOC of unreachable code and make navigation harder for new developers. However, the toolkit/, adapters/, and sources/ hierarchies are well-organized with clean boundaries, preventing the score from dropping lower. Anchored near 70 (clear structural issues with good areas balancing bad).

issues.summary: src/reactive/ directory tree (20 files, 1654 LOC) is almost entirely orphaned with no importers
evidence: event-loop.ts (257 LOC), watermark-store.ts (90 LOC) — orphaned root files
6 of 7 event-handlers are orphaned (all except severity-handler.ts which is imported by the orphaned handlers)
sse-feed.ts (244 LOC), balance-source.ts (104 LOC), storage-watcher.ts (139 LOC) — orphaned event sources
avg fan-in for reactive/ root is 0.3, event-handlers/ is 0.3 — near-zero importers
issues.suggestion: Stage 1: Verify reactive/ is truly unused by checking cli/event-runner.ts imports. Stage 2: If unused, move entire reactive/ to an archive or delete it. Stage 3: If partially used, prune orphaned files and document which files are entry points for the event-driven mode. Run `npx tsx -e 'import * as r from "./src/reactive/event-loop.js"'` to verify import chains.

issues.summary: src/lib/ root has 5+ orphaned files (1268 LOC) that inflate the directory without being imported
evidence: tips.ts (442 LOC) — zero importers in src/
predictions.ts (391 LOC) — zero importers in src/
mentions.ts (148 LOC) — zero importers in src/
transcript.ts (175 LOC) — zero importers in src/
review-findings.ts (112 LOC) — zero importers in src/
Total: 1268 LOC of dead code in the lib/ root
issues.suggestion: Stage 1: Verify these files have zero callers (grep for import paths). Stage 2: Delete orphaned files or move to an archive directory. Stage 3: Consider whether the remaining 9 files in src/lib/ should be further organized (financial: spending-policy.ts + budget-tracker.ts; session: state.ts; config: agent-config.ts).

issues.summary: src/lib/sources/providers/hooks/ has 2 files with zero importers — entire subdirectory is dead
evidence: arxiv.ts (24 LOC) — zero importers confirmed via grep
kraken.ts (140 LOC) — zero importers confirmed via grep
avg fan-in for hooks/ directory is 0.0
issues.suggestion: Delete both files and the hooks/ directory. If the hook pattern is needed in the future, re-implement when there are actual consumers.

root_causes: Speculative implementation of reactive subsystem before integration was complete
Feature code (tips, predictions, mentions) was written but never wired into the session loop or was superseded by toolkit alternatives
likely_symptoms: hooks_dir_fully_orphaned is a symptom of the same pattern as reactive — built but never integrated

add.header: Toolkit directory is well-organized with clear boundaries
add.description: src/toolkit/ has coherent subdirs (tools/, guards/), high fan-in core files, and clean dependency direction (tools/ → toolkit/ → guards/). This is the best-organized subsystem.

add.header: Adapters directory has clean adapter boundaries
add.description: src/adapters/ with eliza/ and skill-dojo/ subdirectories follows proper adapter isolation. Each adapter has its own types.ts and index.ts barrel.

add.header: ~2500 LOC of orphaned code across lib and reactive
add.description: Combined orphaned code in src/lib/ (1268 LOC) and src/reactive/ (~1200 LOC) inflates the codebase by ~10% with unreachable code, hindering navigation.
